valuation.title: Property valuation tool for Madrid
valuation.subtitle: Enter your property characteristics and get an instant valuation based on our AI model, trained with thousands of real market listings.
valuation.how_it_works: How does the valuation tool work?
valuation.how_it_works_p1: Our AI model analyzes thousands of properties for sale in Madrid to estimate the most likely price of your property. It uses a Random Forest algorithm with 200 decision trees, trained with real data and automatically retrained every week to reflect current market conditions.
valuation.how_it_works_p2: The valuation takes into account location (district and neighborhood), floor size, number of rooms, floor level, elevator availability and exterior orientation. Additionally, adjustments are applied for features such as terrace, garage, renovation condition and energy certification, based on typical market premiums in Madrid.
valuation.how_it_works_p3: The result includes a confidence band (10th-90th percentile) that reflects the actual price dispersion in the area. The more comparable properties available, the narrower the band and the more reliable the estimate.
valuation.disclaimer: This valuation is indicative only and does not constitute an official appraisal. It is based on a statistical model trained with listings from real estate portals. For an official valuation, contact a certified appraiser.

footer.source: Source: aggregated Madrid real estate market data

faq.title: Frequently asked questions about Madrid's real estate market
faq.subtitle: Answers updated with real market data
faq.explore_districts: Explore the market by district and neighborhood
faq.explore_subtitle: Detailed data on prices, trends and yield for Madrid's 21 districts and 139 neighborhoods.
faq.q1: How much does a property cost in Madrid?
faq.a1: The median property price in Madrid is {price}, with a variation of {change} compared to the previous period. Prices range from {min_price} in {min_district} to {max_price} in {max_district}.
faq.q2: How much does a square meter cost in Madrid?
faq.a2: The average price per square meter in Madrid is {avg_sqm}. The most expensive district is {most_expensive} ({most_expensive_price}) and the most affordable is {cheapest} ({cheapest_price}).
faq.q3: Is it a good time to buy property in Madrid?
faq.a3: Our market thermometer shows {score}/100 ({label}). A high score (>75) indicates a clearly bullish market; low (<40) suggests opportunities for buyers.
faq.q4: How long does it take to sell a property in Madrid?
faq.a4: The median selling time in Madrid is {days} days.
faq.q5: What is the rental yield in Madrid?
faq.a5: The average gross rental yield in Madrid is {yield}.
faq.q6: How does Euribor affect Madrid's real estate market?
faq.a6: The 12-month Euribor is at {euribor}, with {trend} trend.
faq.q7: What are the most expensive districts in Madrid?
faq.a7: The 5 most expensive Madrid districts by €/m² are: {top_5}.
faq.q8: What are the most affordable districts in Madrid?
faq.a8: The 5 most affordable Madrid districts by €/m² are: {bottom_5}.
faq.q9: What is the notarial gap and what does it indicate?
faq.a9: The notarial gap is the difference between the published price and the price registered at the notary. In Madrid, the average gap is {gap}.
faq.q10: What percentage of properties in Madrid drop in price?
faq.a10: Currently {drop_pct} of properties for sale have experienced at least one price cut.
faq.q11: Where does madridhome.tech data come from?
faq.a11: Data is obtained daily through automated scraping of real estate portals, processed and aggregated statistically.
faq.q12: What does the thermometer score mean?
faq.a12: The thermometer is a 0-100 score summarizing the market state. It combines price trends, sales speed, affordability, Euribor and notarial data.

opendata.source: Source: Madrid City Council — Districts and neighborhoods indicator panel
